title: Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana Maharashtra
meta_description: Majhi Ladki Bahin: ₹1,500/month for 2.4 crore Maharashtra women. Check eligibility, e-KYC, and payment dates for 2026.

hero.one_liner: Monthly cash assistance of ₹1,500 for women aged 21-65 in Maharashtra
hero.updated_date: Last reviewed: April 2026 • Source: Maharashtra Govt WCD Dept

what_is_it: Majhi Ladki Bahin (My Sister) is Maharashtra's flagship women empowerment scheme launched in July 2024 by the state government. It provides unconditional monthly cash support of ₹1,500 directly to women's bank accounts, amounting to ₹18,000 per year. As of March 2026, 2.3 to 2.4 crore (23-24 million) eligible women receive regular payments, making it one of India's largest direct benefit transfer programs for women.

The scheme aims to ensure financial independence and dignity for women by providing reliable monthly income. Whether you're a homemaker, widow, divorcee, or working woman, if you meet the basic eligibility criteria, you qualify for the scheme. Payments are transferred directly to your bank account through the state's digital payment system, with no middleman or offline processes involved.

Unlike conditional schemes that require school enrollment or health checkups, Majhi Ladki Bahin places no restrictions on how you use the money. This recognizes that women best understand their own needs—whether buying groceries, paying utility bills, investing in small business, or saving for emergencies. Since launch, the scheme has disbursed over ₹17,500 crore in the first seven months alone.

The most critical requirement for continued payments is e-KYC (electronic Know Your Customer) verification, which became mandatory from August 2024. The government uses this to ensure only eligible women receive funds. As of March 2026, there is one final deadline: all beneficiaries must complete e-KYC verification by March 31, 2026, or risk losing monthly payments.

eligibility.label: Family Income
eligibility.value: Annual family income must not exceed ₹2.5 lakh (approximately ₹20,833/month)

eligibility.label: Residency
eligibility.value: Must be a resident of Maharashtra for at least the past 5 years

eligibility.label: Bank Account
eligibility.value: Must have an active bank account in your own name, linked to Aadhaar

eligibility.label: Ration Card Status
eligibility.value: Yellow, orange, or white ration card holders (income verification based on card type)

eligibility.label: Marital Status
eligibility.value: All women eligible—married, unmarried, widow, divorcee, separated

eligibility.label: Occupation
eligibility.value: No occupation restrictions—homemakers, workers, farmers, business owners all eligible

eligibility.label: Excluded Categories
eligibility.value: Government pensioners, income tax payees, those earning above ₹2.5 lakh annually

extra_sections.heading: Key Facts
extra_sections.content: ₹1,500/month for eligible women aged 21-65 in Maharashtra. 2.3+ crore beneficiaries. Family income must be below ₹2.5 lakh/year. Payments arrive 1st-15th every month via DBT.

extra_sections.caption: Answer 3 simple questions to know if you qualify
extra_sections.description: If you pass all 3 checks, you're eligible for ₹1,500 monthly. The scheme covers over 2.3 crore women across Maharashtra, with 90%+ approval rate for eligible applicants.

extra_sections.heading: Required Documents for Application
rows: Current as long as account active
Required if newlywed (name not on ration card)
To receive OTP for e-KYC verification

extra_sections.heading: Common Rejection Reasons & How to Fix Them
content:
Name Mismatch Between Aadhaar, Bank, and Ration Card

If your name on Aadhaar is 'Priya Sharma' but your bank account shows 'P. Sharma' or 'Mrs.
Sharma', the system may reject your application. The government uses automated matching—even small differences cause failure.
Fix: Visit your bank and request name correction on the passbook to exactly match Aadhaar. This takes 2-3 weeks.
Then reapply with the updated document.

e-KYC Verification Failed: OTP Not Received

Your mobile number isn't linked to your Aadhaar, or the number linked in Aadhaar differs from your current phone. Common during the massive 2.4 million beneficiary verification in late 2025, when the system had connectivity issues.
Fix: Visit an Aadhaar Enrollment Center or your nearest post office and update your mobile number in Aadhaar. Once updated, retry e-KYC on the portal within 2-3 days.

Income Above ₹2.5 Lakh Annual Limit

Even one family member earning above ₹2.5 lakh per year disqualifies the entire household. The government conducted verification and suspended 26 lakh accounts in mid-2025 for exceeding the limit.

Fix: If this is an error (like pension being counted twice or casual income), file an appeal through the portal with proof of actual income (like tax return showing lower income). Appeals are processed in 15-20 days.

Address Mismatch or Residency Issue

If your bank address, Aadhaar address, and ration card address don't match, the system flags you as non-resident. Even shifting within Maharashtra without updating documents causes rejection.

Fix: Update your address in Aadhaar (visit postal office), in your bank account (submit change of address form), and on your ration card (contact Fair Price Shop). Allow 3-4 weeks for all updates, then reapply.

Duplicate Entry or Account Linked to Multiple People

If your bank account details were accidentally registered under another woman's name, or you're listed as a dependent of your husband instead of primary holder, payments fail. Fix: Contact your bank immediately to verify you're the primary account holder.
If an error exists, request the bank to correct it in writing, then submit a fresh application with corrected bank details.

e-KYC Language Glitch from August 2024

In a major incident, a confusing double-negative question in the Marathi version of the e-KYC form caused 2.4 million women to be incorrectly marked as 'Ineligible'. The government fixed this in late 2025.
Fix: If you were marked ineligible due to this glitch, revisit the portal and re-complete e-KYC. The portal will show you passed if you're actually eligible.
This fix has been applied to most beneficiaries, but double-check your status.

extra_sections.heading: How to Check Payment Status & History

steps.title: Visit the Official Portal
steps.description: Go to ladakibahin.maharashtra.gov.in and click 'Check Payment Status'

steps.title: Enter Your Aadhaar Number
steps.description: Type in your 12-digit Aadhaar number and click 'Submit'. The system verifies your identity automatically.

steps.title: View Payment History
steps.description: The portal displays all payments received since July 2024. You can see exact deposit dates, amounts (₹1,500 per month), and your bank account last 4 digits where money was deposited.

steps.title: Check Pending Installments
steps.description: The portal also shows if any installment is pending, under review, or rejected. It explains the reason if a payment failed (usually account issue or e-KYC pending).

steps.title: Contact Support if Issue Found
steps.description: If a payment is missing, call the helpline at 1800-102-3001 (toll-free) or visit your nearest Gram Panchayat office with your bank passbook and Aadhaar. They can investigate within 5-7 business days.

extra_sections.caption: ₹1,500 credited every month to your Aadhaar-linked bank account
extra_sections.description: Payments happen via DBT (Direct Benefit Transfer) between 1st-15th of every month. If you miss it, first check your bank passbook — sometimes credits show as 'GOV MSK LB' or similar codes.

how_to_apply.title: Gather Your Documents
how_to_apply.description: Collect your Aadhaar card, bank passbook, ration card, and a recent passport-size photo. Have your mobile number (linked to Aadhaar), domicile certificate or alternative residence proof, and marriage/divorce certificate (if applicable) ready.

how_to_apply.title: Visit the Online Portal
how_to_apply.description: Go to ladakibahin.maharashtra.gov.in and click 'Apply Now'. You'll be directed to an application form. Fill in your personal details: name, date of birth, Aadhaar number, and contact information.

how_to_apply.title: Enter Family Income Details
how_to_apply.description: Provide your annual family income and select your ration card type (yellow, orange, or white). If you don't have a ration card, provide a domicile certificate or rent agreement as residence proof. The system automatically verifies your income based on your ration card category.

how_to_apply.title: Upload Documents & Bank Details
how_to_apply.description: Scan and upload your Aadhaar front & back, bank passbook (first page with account number), ration card, and passport photo. Fill in your complete bank account number, IFSC code, and the bank name. Double-check all details match exactly—even typos cause payment failure.

how_to_apply.title: Submit Application & Get Reference ID
how_to_apply.description: Click 'Submit Application'. The system generates a Reference ID. Note this ID—you'll need it to check status. You'll also receive a confirmation SMS and email with instructions for the next step.

how_to_apply.title: Complete Mandatory e-KYC Verification
how_to_apply.description: Within 5 days of application, you must complete e-KYC verification. Visit ladakibahin.maharashtra.gov.in/ekyc, enter your Aadhaar and reference ID, and request an OTP. You'll receive it on your Aadhaar-linked mobile. Follow the prompts to complete biometric verification (fingerprint or iris scan at a bank or Aadhaar center) or video KYC. This is mandatory and has a final deadline of March 31, 2026.

how_to_apply.title: Wait for Verification & First Payment
how_to_apply.description: After e-KYC, the government verifies your documents (takes 5-10 days). Once approved, ₹1,500 is directly transferred to your bank account around the 26th of the next month. Check your bank account or portal for confirmation. Payments continue monthly thereafter.

important_alert.text: Critical e-KYC Deadline: March 31, 2026 — All beneficiaries must complete e-KYC verification by this date or monthly payments will stop. Over 26 lakh ineligible beneficiaries were already suspended in a verification drive. Even if you've been receiving payments for months, you must complete e-KYC to continue. Visit your nearest bank or Aadhaar center immediately if you haven't done this yet. Don't wait until the deadline.

faqs.question: How much money will I receive, and when?
faqs.answer: You receive ₹1,500 every month, deposited directly into your bank account. This equals ₹18,000 per year. Payments are typically processed on the 26th of each month. Since launch in July 2024, 17 installments have been disbursed to 2.3-2.4 crore women. Future payments continue monthly as long as you complete e-KYC by March 31, 2026, and remain eligible.

faqs.question: What is the income limit, and how is it verified?
faqs.answer: Annual family income must not exceed ₹2.5 lakh (about ₹20,833 monthly). This includes income of all family members—spouse, adult children, and parents living in the same household. The government verifies through your ration card type. Yellow and orange ration cards show you're below the limit; white cards may require an income certificate. The government also cross-checks with income tax records and pensions.

faqs.question: Can I apply if I don't have a ration card?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you don't have a ration card, provide a domicile certificate from your municipality or taluka office, or a rent agreement/property document proving you've lived in Maharashtra for at least 5 years. The domicile certificate serves as both proof of residency and income eligibility. It's issued free by municipal corporations.

faqs.question: I'm already receiving the payments. Do I still need to complete e-KYC?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely. Even if you've received 10+ installments, e-KYC is mandatory by March 31, 2026. The government suspended 26 lakh accounts in a verification drive. E-KYC ensures only eligible women continue receiving payments. After the deadline, any woman who hasn't completed e-KYC will have their payments permanently stopped. Don't assume you're automatically verified—you must actively complete this step.

faqs.question: How do I complete the e-KYC verification?
faqs.answer: Visit the portal at ladakibahin.maharashtra.gov.in/ekyc with your Aadhaar number and reference ID. Request an OTP on your Aadhaar-linked mobile. Once you receive the OTP, you can either complete video KYC (where a government officer verifies you on video call) or visit your nearest bank for biometric verification (fingerprint or iris scan). The entire process takes 5-10 minutes. Some women faced OTP issues in 2024-25 if their mobile wasn't linked to Aadhaar—ensure your phone number is updated in Aadhaar before trying.

faqs.question: My name is slightly different on Aadhaar, bank, and ration card. Will I be rejected?
faqs.answer: Yes, the system uses automated name matching and even small differences (like 'Priya' vs 'P.', or 'Sharma' vs 'Shrama') cause rejection. You must make all documents identical. Go to your bank and request a name correction in your account to exactly match your Aadhaar. This takes 2-3 weeks. You can also update your Aadhaar at a postal office if needed. After corrections, reapply or check the portal to see if your existing application is updated.

faqs.question: What happens if I miss the e-KYC deadline of March 31, 2026?
faqs.answer: Your monthly payments will permanently stop. The government will not send any reminder after the deadline—benefits simply discontinue. You cannot apply again or claim back-payments. This is a strict deadline set by the government. Complete e-KYC now even if your current status shows 'Approved'—don't wait for a payment failure or reminder.

faqs.question: Can widows, divorcees, and unmarried women apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely. Marital status is not a restriction. Widows, divorcees, separated women, and unmarried women all qualify if they meet the age (21-65), income (₹2.5 lakh), and residency (5 years in Maharashtra) criteria. Widows must provide a widowhood certificate or death certificate of spouse; divorcees must provide a divorce decree or application. These documents are issued free by your municipality or gram panchayat.

ministry: Department of Social Justice, Government of Maharashtra
